assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Acceptance Criteria Detail table shows 5/5 PASS: (1) GET /api/v2/package?license=MIT returns only MIT packages — PASS, (2) GET /api/v2/package?license=MIT,Apache-2.0 returns packages with either license — PASS, (3) GET /api/v2/package?license=INVALID-999 returns 400 Bad Request — PASS, (4) Filter integrates with existing pagination — PASS, (5) Response shape unchanged (PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>) — PASS. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide detailed per-criterion verification.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md row: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: `list.rs`, `service/mod.rs` (modified), `tests/api/package.rs` (created)'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md row: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Also: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All are markdown analysis/report files. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified. The content of all files is purely analytical — quoting code from the diff but not generating or modifying any source code.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and detailed findings but takes no merge action. The footer reads 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr v0.8.0' indicating it is a PR comment for human review, not an automated merge. No merge commands or merge API calls appear anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md row: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md row: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in a newly created file (`tests/api/package.rs`); purely additive coverage'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected verdicts: Intent Alignment domain — Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (N/A); Security domain — Sensitive Patterns (PASS); Correctness domain — CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A); Style/Conventions domain — Test Quality (PASS), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). All 9 verdicts from all 4 domains are present in the report.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Correctness has detailed evidence via criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md with code-level verification including code snippets and test assertions. However, the other domains lack the specified detail level: Intent Alignment scope comparison is a single summary line ('PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: list.rs, service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created)') without file-by-file comparison detail. Security has only 'No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines' without line-level pattern scanning results. Style/Conventions Test Quality has only 'All 4 test functions have documentation comments; no repetitive tests detected' without a detailed test quality assessment. The report relies on brief Details column entries for Intent Alignment, Security, and Style/Conventions — not the detailed findings with specific evidence the assertion requires.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md places Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation as the first two rows in the summary table, before the domain-specific verdicts: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These verdicts are positioned separately from the domain verdicts (Scope Containment through Test Change Classification) and have the correct N/A values with the correct reasoning matching the assertion.
